fields.title: Make /agents scope-aware for Ralph loop contracts
fields.description: The /agents command currently edits project/session agent selection and has a special project-level Ralph policy. Once Ralph owns its execution contract, /agents must edit the active Ralph loop contract when invoked from a Ralph-owned session.
fields.design: Replace the project-settings Ralph policy path tau.ralph.agents.enabled with loop-state-backed behavior. If the current session is owned by an active Ralph loop, /agents list/enable/disable and the interactive selector read and write that loop state's capabilityContract.agents.enabled list. Normal sessions continue to use project agent settings. UI copy should clearly say whether it is editing project config or the current Ralph loop contract. Keep project config as the initial source used when creating a new Ralph loop, not as the live source for running loops.
fields.acceptance_criteria: /agents list shows Ralph loop scope when invoked inside a Ralph-owned session, enable/disable updates .pi/loops/state/<task-id>.json for that loop, normal sessions still update project settings, and tests cover both scopes without relying on the old tau.ralph.agents.enabled live policy.
